notes:
Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Final action available: record_opening_statement only; no evidence submission in opening.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show four case-packet items, no arguments/openings/submitted evidence yet. Read all four evidence files fully and checked metadata.

Evidence analysis:
- ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page: Kalshi market page extract. Market title: "What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?" Contract family PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION. Strike at issue: Ronaldo. Challenged resolution: No. Requested resolution: Yes. This proves the dispute and the market wording, but not detailed source hierarchy or any exclusions on the market page beyond title/family/strike.
- ev_9cd6fa5297f3_pre-event-reporter-exchange: transcript titled "Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw," Dec. 5, 2025. It places Trump at 11:13 a.m. at the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts. It is a long public reporter exchange about the World Cup draw, host cities, ticket sales, Infantino/FIFA, soccer, etc. It includes the key passage under The President's Interest in Soccer where Trump says he met Ronaldo, came to the White House, was fantastic; says Barron and him had a big conversation; says Barron loves soccer/football; says Ronaldo is great, a great guy, got the whole package, great player. This proves the strike was used as a distinct standalone word by Trump if the exchange is within the relevant time period. Provenance/custody: source URL in file points to presidency.ucsb.edu document; note says transcript released by Office of Communications Dec. 8, 2025, corrected Jan. 2, 2026; portions could not be verified because audio incomplete. Weight issue: audio-incomplete caveat affects inaudibles, but Ronaldo passage itself is transcribed, not marked inaudible.
- ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms: generic NEWMENTION/PRESMENTION terms. Key plaintiff-friendly terms: underlying is words used by entity during time period; time period can be event/context based; word must be distinct standalone unit; event period may include live/pre-recorded segments if official presentation; if event begins but is abbreviated/interrupted, words during occurring portion count; if venue/format/medium changes within window, words stated during event count. Key defense-friendly terms: behind-the-scenes/rehearsals/sound checks/unofficial recordings excluded unless officially broadcast/published as part of the time period; technical details allow Exchange to specify what is included for event periods. This proves no automatic exclusion for event-linked public remarks, but does not by itself define this market's exact event boundary.
- ev_eac0f81b7818_formal-draw-archive-note: note about Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript page for formal attendance remarks. Establishes separate formal archive exists, but the session could not retrieve full text and the note does not establish whether formal program transcript contains Ronaldo. It matters because it highlights the boundary dispute between pre-event reporter exchange and formal program.

Native investigation/source map:
Decisive elements: word utterance; speaker/entity; event/time-period boundary; source agency/provenance; adverse formal-program source.
Searches run: "Donald Trump" "Ronaldo" "FIFA World Cup Draw" "December 5, 2025"; site:presidency.ucsb.edu "Ronaldo" "World Cup Draw" "December 05, 2025"; "Remarks: Donald Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing" "Ronaldo".
Sources/leads identified: White House video page (https://www.whitehouse.gov/videos/president-trump-attends-the-fifa-world-cup-drawing/); Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript (https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-remarks-fifa-world-cup-drawing-december-5-2025/); FOX 5 DC YouTube red-carpet video (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HMW8ryLSsLU); AP and social/video leads. These are not admitted evidence yet and should not be cited as proof in opening.
Adverse check: Opened Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript page. It rendered full transcript lines and visible speakers/timestamps. Browser find for "Ronaldo" and "Cristiano" returned no match. This supports defense theory that formal program transcript may omit the strike, but because not admitted and because plaintiff theory does not require formal program transcript, use only for private framing/source plan.

Opening theory:
The case turns on event boundary, not word-count mechanics. Plaintiff should concede the defense will likely draw a hard line at the formal draw ceremony, but argue the market wording and governing mention terms did not clearly tell traders that the event excluded public reporter/red-carpet/venue remarks immediately before the formal program. Current record supports that Trump said Ronaldo in public remarks at the venue, on World Cup topics, with event actors, before/around the draw. Under preponderance, if the council reads "during the FIFA World Cup Draw" as the event context rather than only formal stage transcript, Yes follows.

Gaps/source plan for later evidence phase: submit primary presidency transcript page if accessible; capture White House video page/FOX 5 red-carpet video or transcript if contains the remarks; submit adverse/formal Roll Call transcript page if needed; inspect full Kalshi market page details for source agency/exclusion language beyond packet extract.

Defendant opening work notes for arb-ex03-20260629194607.

Opportunity: openings:defendant. Prompt and limits reviewed: final action record_opening_statement; text limit 5000; target first submission <=3750 chars; no evidence submission in opening.

Record/evidence inspection: called get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence for all four packet files, and read full byte ranges for all four packet files. No newly submitted evidence; only case-packet evidence visible.

Evidence read:
- ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page: market title, PRESMENTION/NEWMENTION family, strike Ronaldo, challenged resolution No, requested Yes.
- ev_9cd6fa5297f3_pre-event-reporter-exchange: transcript titled 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw.' It contains standalone 'Ronaldo' in a reporter exchange, but title and structure indicate prior-to event/reporter exchange; remarks joined in progress; multiple inaudibles; note says audio incomplete and portions could not be verified; source URL presidency.ucsb.edu; release Dec. 8 2025, corrected Jan. 2 2026.
- ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms: generic mention-market terms. Underlying is words used by entity during time period; time period may be event/context based. For specific events, Exchange may specify included content. Formal/event recording, official presentation, and exclusions for behind-the-scenes/rehearsals/sound checks/unofficial recordings matter. Defense will argue 'during' requires proof the reporter exchange was within the market event period, not merely near/topical.
- ev_eac0f81b7818_formal-draw-archive-note: notes separate Roll Call/Factba.se archive for Trump's formal attendance remarks at the draw; full transcript was not retrieved locally; does not establish whether formal program did or did not contain Ronaldo, but marks key distinction.

Native investigation before filing: web searches for Kalshi title/Ronaldo, exact reporter-exchange title, formal draw transcript. Found govinfo page for DCPD-202501162 (same reporter exchange), Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript lead, Rev formal transcript lead, White House video page, secondary Kalshi dispute roundup. Because opening cannot admit evidence, I will not cite these as record proof. They are source targets for later admission if the argument phase allows submit_evidence.

Adverse/source checks: plaintiff's strongest path is that the red-carpet/pre-program exchange was public, official, event-linked, and possibly livestreamed. If later admitted proof shows it was broadcast/published as part of the World Cup Draw event time period, defense position weakens. Defense source gap: need formal transcript/video and market-specific rule/source details to prove formal draw boundary; current packet itself does not prove formal transcript excludes Ronaldo.

Draft theory: concede word mechanics; challenge market boundary and burden. Plaintiff must prove that the relevant time period included a reporter exchange expressly titled prior to the draw. The packet does not show the market told traders that all venue remarks before the formal program counted; does not supply market-specific source hierarchy/time window beyond generic terms; does not authenticate complete audio for all transcript portions. Opening should be forward-looking and identify decisive later proof: market rules/source page, full formal transcript/video, and official livestream/source-chain for pre-program exchange.

Merits opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Final action submit_argument; submit_evidence allowed.

Record scan: get_case/list_evidence show original four case-packet items unchanged and both openings visible. No new submitted evidence before plaintiff merits. Defendant opening concedes ev_9cd6fa5297f3_pre-event-reporter-exchange contains the standalone word "Ronaldo"; defense contests whether a public reporter exchange before the formal program is inside market time period.

AAR evidence/stat checks: stat_evidence confirmed same metadata and hashes for ev_9cd6fa5297f3, ev_a373371a8c9c, ev_eac0f81b7818. Prior opening read exact bytes of all four case-packet files; contents remain material and unchanged.

Additional investigation/source map:
- Decisive elements: (1) strike utterance, now effectively conceded; (2) speaker/entity; (3) event-boundary/market wording; (4) source provenance and audio/custody weight; (5) adverse formal-program transcript/source.
- Primary source target: American Presidency Project page named in packet transcript. Native curl to https://www.presidency.ucsb.edu/documents/remarks-exchange-with-reporters-prior-the-2026-federation-internationale-des-associations timed out after 20 seconds with 0 bytes. Browser/web retrieval succeeded. Page title: "Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to the 2026 Fédération Internationale des Associations de Football (FIFA) World Cup Draw"; date Dec. 5, 2025. It shows headings and text matching packet, including event headings "2026 World Cup Draw Event," "2026 World Cup Draw Event/John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts," "2026 World Cup Draw/Ticket Sales for World Cup," and material Ronaldo passages: Trump says he got to meet Ronaldo, Ronaldo came to the White House, Ronaldo is great/great player; later says he was not Ronaldo or Messi. This was submitted as ev_c7c5425b23cf_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-c7c5425b23cf, sha256 c7c5425b23cf65ee377a9e441fd03caee1a5163b01726ac5f2d1067bfb11cf6b, 6110 bytes, text/plain.
- Kalshi packet URL opened: https://kalshi.com/markets/kxtrumpmentionb/trump-mention-b/kxtrumpmentionb-25dec06. It rendered market title and rules but selected/displayed the Canada strike, not Ronaldo. Rules text visible included "Resolves Yes if Donald Trump says Canada as part of FIFA World Cup Draw," source agencies, video primarily used, and live televised/streamed press condition. Decision: do not submit now because strike mismatch could confuse the plaintiff merits record. Preserve as lead if defendant later argues market-specific page language; packet already establishes Ronaldo strike and generic terms already admitted.
- White House video page: https://www.whitehouse.gov/videos/president-trump-attends-the-fifa-world-cup-drawing/ opened earlier; page title/date visible, but page text did not contain Ronaldo. Not submitted because without video extraction it does not add source content beyond event existence.
- Adverse Roll Call/Factba.se formal transcript: earlier opened https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-remarks-fifa-world-cup-drawing-december-5-2025/; find for Ronaldo/Cristiano returned no match. Not submitted by plaintiff in merits because plaintiff is not relying on formal program transcript omission; existing ev_eac0f81b7818 already frames the formal-transcript gap.

Evidence analysis for argument:
- ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page proves title, family, Ronaldo strike, challenged No/requested Yes. It does not provide detailed market-specific source hierarchy or exclusion wording.
- ev_9cd6fa5297f3_pre-event-reporter-exchange proves the transcript source content, event/venue context, and Ronaldo passages. It carries provenance note/correction/audio incomplete caveat; caveat affects inaudibles but not the plainly transcribed Ronaldo passages.
- ev_c7c5425b23cf submitted APP extract corroborates packet transcript with primary public page text and multiple draw-event headings around the remarks.
- ev_a373371a8c9c terms support broad event/context reading and standalone word rule; also contain exclusions defense will emphasize. Plaintiff response: exclusions are for behind-the-scenes/rehearsal/sound-check/unofficial material, not public reporter Q/A at the event venue on draw topics, captured in primary public transcript.
- ev_eac0f81b7818 formal archive note proves only that separate formal transcript exists and was not retrieved; it does not prove formal transcript controls or that event-linked public remarks are excluded.

Planned merits theory: The evidence more likely than not satisfies Yes because the word was uttered by Trump in an officially documented public exchange at the draw venue, surrounded by World Cup Draw headings/content, and the market wording did not clearly impose the defendant's formal-stage-only limitation. The defense's best point is the title "Prior to" and the terms' event-scope provisions. Answer: "prior to" means prior to formal stage program, not outside the market event context; the burden is not to prove formal transcript contains Ronaldo, but to show Kalshi's No improperly excluded event-linked remarks under ambiguous wording.

Defendant merits work notes for arb-ex03-20260629194607.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant. Final action: submit_argument. Evidence submission allowed. Limits reviewed: text 6000 chars, up to 8 submitted evidence per side, 12 exhibits, 4 reports.

Record scan: get_case and list_evidence showed plaintiff argument plus new submitted evidence ev_c7c5425b23cf_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-c7c5425b23cf. Stat/read performed for that evidence. It is an APP focused extract of the same pre-event reporter exchange. It improves provenance for plaintiff on the exchange but also preserves the title 'Remarks in an Exchange With Reporters Prior to...' and the provenance caveat: released Dec. 8 2025, corrected Jan. 2 2026, portions could not be verified because audio incomplete. It confirms no new market-specific rule that pre-program exchange is inside time period.

Native investigation: Opened Rev formal transcript page at https://www.rev.com/transcripts/fifa-world-cup-drawing. Local Python stdlib fetch with Mozilla UA returned HTTP 200, HTML length 132861, extracted text length 56928. Case-insensitive text search for 'ronaldo' found 0 matches. The page is titled 'FIFA World Cup Drawing,' describes Trump attending the FIFA World Cup Drawing and FIFA Peace Prize ceremony, and includes timestamped formal program transcript. Also opened Roll Call/Factba.se and White House video leads earlier; Roll Call was accessible in web open and shows formal transcript metadata; Rev had a readable transcript usable for source submission. No use of credentials/private sources.

Evidence submitted by defense: ev_177c67332111_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-177c67332111, title 'Rev transcript extract: formal FIFA World Cup Drawing program,' accepted. It contains a focused formal-program transcript extract, retrieval/provenance details, and a local text-search note that fetched page text had zero 'ronaldo' matches. This source is not official like APP, but it is directly relevant as a formal-program comparator and as evidence of the distinction between formal program and reporter exchange.

Analysis: Plaintiff now clearly proves Trump said standalone 'Ronaldo' in the pre-event reporter exchange. Defense should not contest word mechanics. The dispute is whether plaintiff proves the market's time period included that exchange. Evidence against inclusion: market title says during the FIFA World Cup Draw; reporter source title says prior to the draw; terms treat specific-event scope, source agency, official presentation, live stream/broadcast, and behind-the-scenes/unofficial material as meaningful; formal-program comparator contains no Ronaldo. Evidence for plaintiff: APP headings call portions World Cup Draw Event; exchange occurred at Kennedy Center, public, official transcript, with FIFA president, and on event subject. Missing link remains market-specific rule/source evidence or official recording/livestream showing the reporter exchange was part of the covered event/presentation.

Planned filing: offer DX-1 = ev_177... formal transcript comparator, DX-2 = ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms, DX-3 = ev_eac0... formal archive note, and possibly DX-4 = plaintiff APP extract for adverse/provenance/title point. Include technical report summarizing source-search ledger and limitations. Argument to council: plaintiff bears preponderance on boundary; event adjacency is insufficient; No resolution should stand absent proof that the prior reporter exchange was within the market time period.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Final action submit_rebuttal/pass. Record scanned with list_evidence; new defendant evidence identified and read fully.

New defense evidence: ev_177c67332111_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-177c67332111, 'Rev transcript extract: formal FIFA World Cup Drawing program,' source_url https://www.rev.com/transcripts/fifa-world-cup-drawing, sha256 177c6733211159ba2d1193689bf2f513445144f83bf732788a5ef72753122980, 7153 bytes. Read full content via read_evidence_range. Evidence says Rev page title 'FIFA World Cup Drawing,' publication date Dec. 8, 2025, focused extract includes formal peace-prize and draw-assistant program. It includes local search note: fetched Rev page text contained zero case-insensitive matches for 'ronaldo.'

Defense argument to answer: plaintiff proved Trump said Ronaldo but not that the prior reporter exchange was within 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw.' Defense uses APP title 'Prior to...' and Rev formal-program comparator lacking Ronaldo.

Analysis of defense evidence:
- Rev extract proves at most that one formal-program transcript/extract lacks Ronaldo. It does not prove market scope, does not define Kalshi's time period, and does not say the pre-program public reporter exchange was excluded.
- Rev is secondary transcript evidence, not Kalshi's market rule and not a source agency hierarchy document for this dispute.
- Rev actually undercuts a rigid boundary: its formal 'FIFA World Cup Drawing' transcript includes several pre-draw components before the literal draw begins: peace-prize/certificate remarks starting 00:16; Trump award remarks at 03:55; Infantino at 16:04 saying 'we are now going to do the draw,' after those earlier segments. Thus even the formal comparator treats the event as broader than the mechanical draw. That supports plaintiff's point that 'during the FIFA World Cup Draw' can include official/event-adjacent segments and should not be narrowed silently to the draw mechanics.
- Defendant's best point remains 'prior to' in APP title. Response: 'prior to the formal program' does not equal outside the market event context; APP page itself uses multiple World Cup Draw Event headings and places the exchange at the venue with Infantino, on draw topics.

No new rebuttal evidence submitted. Existing admitted record plus defendant's own evidence suffice. Kalshi page opened earlier rendered a Canada strike on same event URL, so not submitted to avoid confusing the strike-specific record.

Rebuttal plan: concise. Focus on: (1) defendant asks for a market-specific exclusion that is absent; plaintiff burden is preponderance under ambiguous wording; (2) Rev absence of Ronaldo is non-dispositive because formal transcript is comparator, not boundary rule; (3) Rev shows event itself includes pre-draw ceremonial segments, so 'during' is not literal draw-only; (4) terms' exclusions are for backstage/rehearsal/sound-check/unofficial recordings, not this public official transcript; (5) if Kalshi meant formal transcript only, it needed to say so.

Defendant surrebuttal notes for arb-ex03-20260629194607.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Final actions allowed: submit_surrebuttal or pass. Evidence submission technically allowed, but plaintiff rebuttal added no new evidence and raised an interpretive point, so no new source search or evidence submission needed.

Record scan: called get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence for defendant Rev exhibit ev_177c67332111_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-177c67332111. Evidence list unchanged from merits: packet files, plaintiff APP extract, defendant Rev formal-program extract. Plaintiff rebuttal added only a technical report reviewing defendant Rev extract.

Plaintiff rebuttal strongest point: accepts Rev formal-program extract lacks Ronaldo, but argues the formal 'FIFA World Cup Drawing' program itself includes peace-prize/certificate remarks before Infantino says 'now going to do the draw,' so the event is broader than literal draw mechanics. Plaintiff says if the formal program boundary is broad, pre-program public reporter exchange should count absent an express exclusion.

Defense answer: concede the event is broader than the seconds of drawing balls. But the defense never required literal draw mechanics only; it drew the boundary at official program/presentation versus a separate reporter exchange whose source title says 'Prior to...' The Rev exhibit remains useful because it shows a formal program/presentation artifact with opening ceremony, award, Trump's formal answer, and draw mechanics, all distinct from APP's prior reporter exchange. The terms require words during specified time period and recognize official presentation/source boundaries; exclusion categories do not create a rule that any public event-adjacent remarks count if not specifically excluded. Plaintiff still lacks market-specific proof that the 11:13 a.m. reporter availability was included in the market time period.

Planned surrebuttal: short, directly answer plaintiff's breadth argument, cite record evidence already offered, no new exhibits needed or possibly re-offer key exhibits if helpful. Keep under 4000 chars.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Final action deliver_closing_statement only; no evidence submission allowed.

Final record check: get_case and list_evidence reviewed. Evidence list unchanged after defendant surrebuttal: ev_2c7c2c8a2d76_market-page; ev_9cd6fa5297f3_pre-event-reporter-exchange; ev_a373371a8c9c_presmention-terms; ev_eac0f81b7818_formal-draw-archive-note; plaintiff ev_c7c5425b23cf_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-c7c5425b23cf; defendant ev_177c67332111_submitted-evidence-02-defendant-177c67332111. Stat checks confirmed plaintiff APP extract and defendant Rev extract metadata. No new evidence in surrebuttal.

Defendant surrebuttal point: defense no longer argues literal ball-draw mechanics only. It argues the covered boundary is formal program/presentation versus separate reporter exchange titled 'Prior to' the draw. It emphasizes plaintiff has no admitted market-specific inclusion of reporter availability.

Closing analysis:
- Concessions to make: Rev formal-program transcript lacks Ronaldo; APP/reporter exchange title says prior to draw; no admitted Kalshi rule page explicitly says pre-program reporter exchanges count.
- Plaintiff's decisive evidence: market title says 'What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?' and not 'formal program transcript only'; APP + packet show public official transcript at Kennedy Center with Infantino, on draw topics, under World Cup Draw Event headings; Trump says Ronaldo multiple times plainly; terms make distinct word count easy and exclude backstage/rehearsal/sound-check/unofficial recordings, not public officially transcribed reporter exchange.
- Response to burden framing: Defendant asks for an express inclusion clause, but Kalshi's No resolution needs a clear exclusionary boundary. Under preponderance, public event-linked remarks at the venue immediately before formal program fall within broader event/context wording more likely than not.
- Defense Rev comparator proves only absence from one formal program artifact. It does not define market scope and cannot supply the missing exclusion rule. It also shows the formal program itself included ceremony/remarks before the actual draw, so event boundaries are not as rigid as defendant suggests.

Draft closing theory: The council should resolve Yes because the only disputed issue is scope, and the admitted record shows the scope was not clearly narrowed to exclude a public official transcript of draw-venue remarks tied to the event. The word was said; the terms do not bar it; the No resolution rests on an unstated formal-only limitation.

Defendant closing work notes for arb-ex03-20260629194607.

Opportunity: closings:defendant. Final action: deliver_closing_statement. Evidence submission and technical reports not allowed in closing; use existing record only. Text limit 5000.

Record scan: called list_evidence after defendant closing became ready. Same six visible evidence items: defendant Rev extract ev_177c673..., market page ev_2c7..., packet reporter transcript ev_9cd..., terms ev_a373..., plaintiff APP extract ev_c7c..., formal archive note ev_eac.... No new evidence after surrebuttal.

Plaintiff closing strongest points: word mechanics satisfied; market title says during FIFA World Cup Draw not formal Rev transcript; APP/packet are public official remarks at Kennedy Center with Infantino and repeated World Cup Draw headings; terms exclude backstage/rehearsals/sound checks/unofficial recordings and the APP exchange is none of those; defendant Rev formal program proves only absence from one comparator and not exclusion from the market.

Defense closing response: concede Ronaldo in APP/reporter exchange; focus on burden and inclusion. The proposition requires Yes because the market wording plus terms did not clearly exclude event-linked remarks, but plaintiff must still prove remarks are within the specified time period. Absence of an express exclusion is not proof of inclusion. Market page does not expand to pre-program press; APP title says 'Prior to'; formal Rev program is a coherent broader event/presentation with no Ronaldo; terms recognize official presentation/source boundaries. Plaintiff's facts prove topic, venue, public nature, and official transcript, not market-period inclusion.

Planned closing: concise synthesis, confront adverse point directly, ask council to uphold No.
